chunk_txt: Q1: Compare between somatic cells and gametes. Answer: Somatic cells: All cells of the living organism, except the reproductive cells; Produced by mitosis of somatic cells; Contains the complete chromosome count (2n) in the form of homologous pairs; Symbolized by (2n). Examples: Parenchymal cells, intestinal cells. Gametes: Specialized cells responsible for sexual reproduction; Produced by meiosis of gonad cells; Contains half the chromosome count (n) present in somatic cells; Symbolized by (n). Examples: Ovules, pollen grains, ova, sperms.

chunk_txt: Law of levers: The levers shown in the opposite figures are described as being at equilibrium (in balance) because they verify the law of levers. Law of levers: The product of multiplying the effort by its arm equals the product of multiplying the load by its arm. The product of multiplying the effort by its arm is called moment (torque) of effort, and the product of multiplying the load by its arm is called moment (torque) of load. i.e. The lever is at equilibrium (balanced) when moment of effort equals moment of load.

chunk_txt: Net force determines the kinematic state of the object. When the net force equals zero (balanced forces), a stationary object remains at rest, and a moving object continues moving at the same constant velocity. When the net force does not equal zero (unbalanced forces), a stationary object moves in the direction of the net force. For a moving object, velocity increases if the direction of the net force is in the same direction of its motion, velocity decreases if the direction of the net force is opposite to the direction of its motion, and the direction of motion changes if the direction of the net force is at an angle to the direction of its motion.

chunk_txt: Endothermic reactions require the absorption of thermal energy from the surroundings, causing the temperature of the surroundings to decrease. Examples: Decomposition reaction of calcium carbonate, decomposition reaction of ozone gas in the atmosphere, reaction of sodium bicarbonate with dilute acids. chunk_txt: Exothermic reactions are represented by an energy diagram showing the energy level of reactants higher than the energy level of products. From this diagram, it is clear that the amount of energy released during the formation of new bonds in product molecules is greater than the amount of energy absorbed during the breakage of the bonds in reactant molecules. The difference between the amounts of released energy and absorbed energy appears as released thermal energy from the reaction, thus fulfilling the law of conservation of energy.

subject: science
chunk_txt: The forces acting on an object may be balanced forces or unbalanced forces. If two forces are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction along the same line of action, the net force is the difference between the two forces, which equals zero, and these are known as balanced forces. If several forces act along the same line of action, and they are in the same direction, the net force is the sum of these forces in their common direction. If they are not equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, the net force is the difference between these forces and in the direction of the greater force. In these cases, the net force does not equal zero, and they are known as unbalanced forces.

unit_name: Thermal Energy and Changes of Matter
chunk_txt: Combustion reactions are known as oxidation-reduction (redox) reactions. When a stream of air passes over red-hot copper, copper combines with oxygen to form black copper oxide (2Cu + O2 -> 2CuO), and copper undergoes an oxidation process. When a stream of hydrogen gas passes over black copper oxide with heating, reddish-brown copper is formed (CuO + H2 -> Cu + H2O), and copper oxide undergoes a reduction process. Oxidation is a process in which the percentage of oxygen increases or hydrogen decreases. Reduction is a process in which the percentage of oxygen decreases or hydrogen increases. The substance that undergoes oxidation is the reducing agent, and the substance that undergoes reduction is the oxidizing agent.

chunk_txt: Comparison between exothermic reactions and endothermic reactions: Exothermic reactions release thermal energy to the surroundings, causing the temperature of the surroundings to increase. Examples: Reactions of metals with dilute acids, reactions of alkalis with acids, reactions of metal carbonates with dilute acids. The energy diagram shows reactants at a higher energy level than products. The amount of energy released during forming new bonds in product molecules is greater than the amount of energy absorbed during breakage of the bonds in the reactant molecules. Therefore, the reaction (system) releases thermal energy equal to the difference between the amount of released energy and absorbed energy, thus fulfilling the law of conservation of energy.

chunk_txt: When it is said in a weather forecast for one day that the weather is hot and humid over Cairo and the Nile Delta (Lower Egypt), very hot and humid over South Sinai and the south of the country during the day and warm and humid at night over most areas, so it talks about weather. But when it is said that the Mediterranean coastal countries have a hot dry climate in summer and a mild, rainy climate in winter, then it talks about climate. Weather is the overall instantaneous condition of the atmosphere at certain place and time. Climate is the atmospheric condition in a certain place over a long period of time.
